findings_details.title: shfmt formatting violations in pr-verdict.sh and test_pr_verdict.sh
findings_details.body: gate-lint bin-format check fails: both scripts need shfmt formatting. Fix: run bin/format and commit.

findings_details.title: noqa comment in fixture body triggers lint-suppressions gate
findings_details.body: verbose-cr.json fixture body contains '# noqa: F401' inside a Python code example string. The lint-suppressions gate scans the entire diff including JSON string contents and matches this as a real suppression. The fixture is a test data file — the noqa comment is inside a quoted Python snippet, not real code. Fix: either escape/reword the noqa comment in the fixture (e.g. 'noqa:F401' without the space, or rephrase) or add a lint allowlist entry for this fixture with a precise reason.

findings_details.title: pre_merge loop breaks on first CodeRabbit check, missing worse-severity checks
findings_details.body: Lines 232-243: the loop iterates contexts and breaks immediately after the first node whose name starts with 'CodeRabbit'. If a repo has multiple CodeRabbit check-runs (e.g. 'CodeRabbit Review' NEUTRAL then 'CodeRabbit Pre-merge Checks' FAILURE), only the first is evaluated; the error is silently dropped and pre_merge=warning rather than error. Fix: remove the `break`; instead track the worst pre_merge value seen across all CodeRabbit checks (error > warning > green). No test fixture covers this multi-CodeRabbit-check scenario.

findings_details.title: python3 -c interpolates $binding path — breaks if repo path contains a single quote
findings_details.body: Line 66: repo=$(python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(open('$binding')); ..."). If REPO_ROOT contains a single-quote (e.g. /Users/O'Brien/...), the Python literal is malformed and the command silently returns empty, causing the function to return 1 with a misleading error. Fix: pass the path via environment variable — export _BINDING_PATH="$binding" and use python3 -c 'import json,os; d=json.load(open(os.environ["_BINDING_PATH"])); ...' so no shell interpolation occurs inside the Python string.

findings_details.title: _assert_contains called with desc==needle — test description is not human-readable
findings_details.body: Lines 126-130: _run_fixture_test passes the assertion expression itself as both desc and needle (e.g. _assert_contains "verdict=READY" "verdict=READY" "$output"). The description field should be prose that explains what is being asserted, e.g. 'verdict field matches expected value'. Failure messages currently say 'FAIL: verdict=READY' which reads as a test named after its own assertion.

findings_details.title: _nitpick_count uses max() instead of sum() for mixed prefix+emoji bodies
findings_details.body: When a CodeRabbit CHILL-profile body contains both 'Nitpick:' prefixes and '🧹 Nitpick' emoji-prefixed lines (without a 'Nitpick comments (N)' heading), line 210 does `max(prefix_count, emoji_count)` instead of summing the two. A body with 2 prefix-style and 3 emoji-style nitpicks returns 3 instead of 5, causing actionable undercount and a false READY verdict. Fix: replace `max(prefix_count, emoji_count)` with `prefix_count + emoji_count`. The heading-based path (strategy 1) remains correct because it reads the author-supplied total directly. The catch-all (strategy 4) is only reached when nitpick_count==0 so it is not affected.

findings_details.title: tail -n 1 on empty log file silently returns empty string — handled but worth documenting
findings_details.body: Line 27: `LAST=$(tail -n 1 "$LOG")`. If the log file exists but is completely empty (e.g. created by a failed write), `tail -n 1` returns an empty string. Lines 34-37 check for empty VERDICT_SHA/FETCHED_AT and exit 2 with 'pr-verdict log corrupt', which is the correct behavior. No code change needed, but the test suite has no test for the empty-file case. A test with a zero-byte log file would confirm this path is exercised.

findings_details.title: No test for empty (zero-byte) log file
findings_details.body: _write_verdict_log always writes a non-empty line. The code path for an existing-but-empty log file (LAST is empty string, exits 2 with 'log corrupt') is untested. Add a test that touches the log file empty and asserts exit 2 + stderr contains 'log corrupt'.

findings_details.title: Integration test verifies block surfaced but not the specific message
findings_details.body: Lines 176-184: the integration test only checks whether stdout contains 'permissionDecision' OR stderr contains 'pr-verdict'. It does not assert on the specific error message ('no fresh pr-verdict'). This means a guard that blocks for any reason (e.g. block-merge-without-ci firing first) would pass the test even if enforce-pr-verdict never ran. Minor — the integration test is by design a smoke test — but noting it for completeness.
